items.title: Jido.AI LLM Backend Boundary And API Compatibility

requirements.statement: Retrieval and quota actions shall expose direct in-process memory and budget primitives that can be used standalone or behind plugins.

Jido.AI shall provide standalone action modules for LLM generation, tool orchestration, planning, retrieval, quota, and reasoning execution so AI work can run via Jido.Exec without a long-lived agent.
Tool-calling actions and turn helpers shall normalize tool schemas, execute registered Jido.Action tools, and project assistant/tool follow-up messages for iterative loops.
Backend selection for request-bearing entrypoints shall remain additive and explicit rather than replacing the current ask, await, generate, or stream API surface.

Jido.AI shall expose direct LLM facade entrypoints with explicit model alias resolution and merged runtime defaults for text, object, and streaming generation, while backend execution changes stay behind those stable entrypoint contracts.
Jido.AI.Agent shall remain the default ReAct-first long-lived agent surface with explicit ask/await/ask_sync orchestration and request-scoped runtime overrides.
Request tracking shall isolate concurrent runs through per-request handles, status correlation, streaming sinks, and await semantics instead of a single mutable result slot.
Repo-owned tests shall capture executable regression coverage for current-truth contracts as new internal seams or error boundaries are introduced.
Repo-owned quality checkpoint helpers shall define canonical fast/full gate command sets and traceability closure utilities for release hygiene.
Runnable agents, tools, skills, and demo scripts shall live in the top-level examples/ tree and stay out of the core package compile path by default.
Model routing, policy, retrieval, and quota plugins shall provide deterministic cross-cutting runtime behavior through stable signal-route and plugin-state contracts.
Default Jido.AI agent composition shall include TaskSupervisor, Policy, and ModelRouting, with Retrieval and Quota enabled explicitly through plugin-stack options.

The public plugin surface shall center on Chat, Planning, and per-strategy reasoning plugins, while TaskSupervisor remains internal runtime infrastructure rather than a public capability recommendation.
The current plugin and signal surface shall keep the v3 migration contract explicit for users upgrading from the removed v2 public plugins.

Backend-specific provider, CLI session, tool, and stream semantics shall be normalized into canonical Jido.AI directives, signals, runtime events, and turn inputs before strategy logic or public runtime consumers depend on them.
Canonical signal namespaces and telemetry event paths shall stay stable across strategies, runtime, and tooling, with normalized metadata and measurements.
Directives shall emit standardized request, llm, tool, and embed signal/result envelopes with explicit correlation, retry, and timeout semantics.
Runtime flow shall keep strategy policy, directive intent, side-effect execution, and signal routing as explicit layers so bugs can be fixed at the correct boundary.
User-facing error output shall remain sanitized while richer detail stays available for logs, telemetry, and runtime debugging.
Jido.AI shall keep an AI-specific structured error taxonomy and recovery model across provider, validation, tool, backend capability, and unknown failure classes.
Validation and sanitization surfaces shall enforce bounded prompts, turns, callbacks, and telemetry redaction before untrusted input reaches runtime or user-visible channels.
Skill registry behavior shall support explicit startup, lazy ensure_started safety, registration, lookup, unregister, and teardown without fragile startup ordering.
mix jido_ai.skill shall provide list, show, and validate flows with predictable usage, strict, and json behavior for skill files and directories.
The skills system shall support both module-defined skills and runtime-loaded SKILL.md skills through one manifest/body/allowed-tools/actions/plugins abstraction.
Jido.AI shall provide built-in ReAct, CoD, CoT, AoT, ToT, GoT, TRM, and Adaptive reasoning families through convenience agent macros and strategy modules.
Strategy choice shall be an explicit workload-level decision with documented defaults, recipes, and tradeoffs rather than an implicit prompt tweak.
Jido.AI.Reasoning.ReAct shall remain a standalone streaming and checkpoint-aware runtime that can run, start, continue, collect, cancel, steer, and inject outside agent macros.
Strategy implementations shall keep orchestration, machine, worker, and result-shape contracts explicit so strategy-specific behavior stays separate from app-level action APIs.
Jido.AI.Context shall support explicit append, import, restore, and projection semantics for user, assistant, and tool messages with optional system prompt handling.
ReAct shall keep an append-only core thread log and a deterministic materialized Jido.AI.Context projection for LLM-visible conversation state.
Jido.AI.Turn shall normalize provider or backend responses, extracted text, tool calls, usage metadata, and assistant/tool follow-up messages into one canonical turn contract.
Configuration documentation shall keep model aliases, llm defaults, strategy defaults, request defaults, security defaults, and CLI defaults explicit instead of implicit runtime convention.
mix jido_ai.install shall integrate with Igniter when available to seed base configuration and otherwise fail clearly with install guidance.
mix jido_ai shall support one-shot, stdin, existing-agent, and strategy-adapter workflows with explicit output-format, timeout, and trace behavior.
jido_ai shall expose both long-lived agent surfaces and reusable action or runtime surfaces so AI behavior can run inside Jido agents or inside direct workflow execution paths.

jido_ai shall keep model routing, timeout, retry, structured I/O, and tool policy explicit while keeping provider-specific or CLI-runtime-specific behavior behind explicit LLM backend integration boundaries.
Cross-cutting LLM backend evolution shall preserve the previous-version public API surface, including stable entrypoint names, arities, return-shape contracts, and canonical signal/event contracts.
jido_ai shall provide the AI runtime layer for Jido for defining tools and agents and for running synchronous or asynchronous requests with request orchestration and observability.
The repository shall maintain a package-local .spec workspace for current-truth specs, durable ADRs, phased implementation planning, and generated spec state aligned with code, guides, and tests.
The repository shall include a .spec/AGENTS.md that tells agents to start a session with mix spec.prime before editing current truth.
The repository shall include a .spec/AGENTS.md that gives local operating guidance for agents working inside the .spec workspace.
The repository shall include a .spec/decisions/README.md that explains when durable ADRs belong in the workspace.
The workspace may keep phased implementation and migration plans under .spec/planning/, while current-truth rules remain in .spec/specs and .spec/decisions.
The repository shall include a .spec/README.md that explains purpose, layout, and workflow.
When indexing and validation run, the workspace shall generate .spec/state.json containing indexed subjects, indexed decisions, and verification state.

meta.summary: Built-in standalone actions cover LLM generation, tool calling, planning, retrieval, quota, and reasoning execution outside long-lived agents.

Public runtime entrypoints expose direct LLM facades, a default ReAct-first agent macro, and request-handle orchestration for concurrent AI work while backend execution stays behind stable request contracts.
Example agents and demo scripts stay outside the core compile path, while repo-owned quality helpers define repeatable gate and traceability checks.

jido_ai is the Jido ecosystem AI runtime layer for tool-using agents, reusable actions, direct LLM facade calls, and explicit reasoning strategy execution through stable public APIs with explicit LLM backend boundaries.
Public capability plugins package chat, planning, and strategy invocation while cross-cutting plugins handle routing, policy, retrieval, quota, and internal task-supervisor support.
Runtime execution keeps strategy, directives, side effects, signals, and telemetry as explicit layers with canonical envelopes and event names.
Validation, sanitization, telemetry redaction, and structured AI error handling provide defense-in-depth across user input, runtime failures, and recovery decisions.
The skills system unifies module-defined and SKILL.md-loaded skills behind one manifest/body/actions/plugins API with registry-backed runtime resolution.
Canonical workspace contract for authored specs, durable ADRs, optional planning docs, and generated Spec Led state.
Jido.AI ships multiple reasoning strategy families, convenience agent macros, standalone ReAct runtime, and explicit strategy internals instead of prompt-only orchestration.
ReAct keeps append-only thread truth, deterministic Jido.AI.Context projection, and canonical Jido.AI.Turn normalization for follow-up execution.
Repo-owned tooling keeps configuration defaults explicit, exposes shell-first CLI workflows, and provides an Igniter-based install path when available.
